name: Token budget — Context pressure regression threshold
hypothesis: At some breadcrumb density, the added context tokens begin to crowd out other important context (long results.jsonl, large codebase), leading to quality REGRESSIONS. This scenario tests where that threshold is.

setup.description: Simulate a session with a very large codebase (multiple files) and a very long results.jsonl (20+ experiments). Add increasingly dense breadcrumbs until we can measure quality degradation. Uses synthetic 'large session' fixtures.

stimulus.system_prompt: You are resuming an autoresearch session to optimize a multi-file string processing pipeline.
stimulus.user_prompt: Resume the autoresearch session. Read autoresearch.md and .autoresearch/results.jsonl. Then read all source files. Propose the next 3 experiments with full experiment cards.

measurement.plot: HNS and RR as a function of breadcrumb_density
measurement.find_inflection_point: At what TOR value does HNS begin to decrease or RR begin to increase?

pass_criteria.note: No binary pass/fail. Produces a threshold curve: 'breadcrumbs up to TOR=X improve quality; beyond that, quality degrades'.
pass_criteria.expected_finding: HNS peaks at some intermediate TOR, then decreases as breadcrumb tokens crowd out context. The optimal TOR is the inflection point.

meta_recommendation: Use this scenario's output to set a CHUNK_SIZE equivalent for breadcrumbs — a maximum comment density beyond which further breadcrumbs should be moved to DECISION.md instead.
